methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $134M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $2.5B.

data.narrative:
Reliable Robotics is a Series C-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Robert Rose and Juerg Frefel, having raised $134M to date. The valuation analysis employs two applicable methodologies against a comparable universe of 5 private peers at identical stage and sector (Axiom Space, Impulse Space, Varda Space Industries, Skyryse, Isar Aerospace) and 5 public Space & Aerospace comps.

Stage-progression methodology applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier to the $134M total raised proxy, yielding $228M. Peer-median analysis anchors to the $1.5B median valuation across the 5 same-stage, same-sector private comparables, with a range of $1.0B to $2.5B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue data, typical for unlisted companies.

Triangulation of the two applicable methods produces a valuation range of $194M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $864M. The wide spread reflects material divergence between stage-based and peer-median approaches. The stage-progression method understates peer valuations substantially; peer-median likely better reflects current market clearing for Series C aerospace platforms, though Reliable Robotics' specific operational metrics, capital efficiency, and technology differentiation relative to the peer set remain key uncertainties. Revenue, unit economics, and customer pipeline visibility would narrow this range materially.
